title: Deschampsia refracta
taxonomy: Deschampsia refracta is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Deschampsia, which includes several other species of grasses. The genus Deschampsia is a part of the tribe Poeae, which is a large group of grasses. Deschampsia refracta is native to certain regions and is often used as an ornamental plant due to its unique characteristics. Its classification also reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses in the same family and genus.

intro: Deschampsia refracta, also known as Fringed Brome or Refracta Grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Deschampsia refracta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Deschampsia refracta is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and skin problems.
